title: sorbus cashmiriana var. aitchisonii
intro: Sorbus cashmiriana var. aitchisonii, also known as Himalayan Rowan, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to the Himalayan region, characterized by its attractive white flowers and red berries. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine, with its bark, leaves, and roots possessing antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. The medicinal properties of Sorbus cashmiriana var. aitchisonii offer various health benefits, including relief from fever, headaches, and respiratory issues, as well as protection against bacterial and fungal infections. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared into decoctions, infusions, and ointments to harness its medicinal properties, making it a valuable resource in folk medicine.
